concepts.lesson: Statistical data consists of numerical information collected for a specific purpose. Data may be primary or secondary and is analysed to identify patterns, trends, and relationships.
The mean is the average value of a data set and is calculated by dividing the sum of all observations by the number of observations. It provides a central value but is sensitive to extreme values.
concepts.real_example: Average marks scored by a student.

concepts.lesson: The median is the middle value of a data set arranged in order. It divides the data into two equal parts and is less affected by extreme values.
The mode is the value that appears most frequently in a data set. It is useful for identifying common trends, especially in qualitative data.
Graphs such as bar graphs, histograms, and ogives visually represent data, making it easier to interpret trends and comparisons. Graphical methods are essential for data analysis.
